Title: AT&S invests up to €2bil in Malaysia plan to capitalise on AI boom
Article:
VIENNA: Austrian circuit board manufacturer AT&S has announced it is investing up to €2 billion (US$2.3 billion) in Malaysia to capitalise on the AI boom, sending its shares soaring nearly 30 per cent.
"We will fully expand our site in Kulim," the company's chier executive officer Michael Mertin told Reuters in an interview on Monday.
The company announced on Saturday it would expand capacity in Kulim and raised its outlook for 2026/27, citing agreements with its customer AMD, the US chipmaker, and another technology company on the back of strong demand for AI infrastructure.
AT&S did not disclose the name of its second major client, while industry sources say it is Intel.
AT&S said the investments required for additional production capacity at the existing plant and in the previously unused building at the second plant in Kulim were fully supported and financed by long-term customer commitments.
"We expect to have represented at least five of the leading high-tech clients we know as tech partners from the U.S.," said Mertin.
Shares were trading up 28.5 per cent at €199 at 1009 GMT, after hitting a record high of €200 earlier in the day.
Mertin said it was likely the company would not pay a dividend for the current and coming year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MACC to charge individual with 158 offences linked to Op Sutra
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) will charge an individual at the Shah Alam Sessions Court tomorrow with 158 counts linked to the misappropriation of public funds under "Op Sutra".
MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman said the individual would be charged under Section 23 of the MACC Act 2009.
He said investigations found that the suspect's alleged modus operandi involved setting up several companies for personal benefit and that of other parties.
"The individual will also be charged next week at the Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court on several counts related to money laundering activities under the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act (Amla)," he said.
He told Bernama and TV3 this after visiting the Sabah MACC office at the Federal Government Administrative Complex here today. Also present was Sabah MACC director Datuk Mohd Fuad Bee Basrah.
Meanwhile, Abdul Halim said the chief executive officer of a statutory body, who is suspected of involvement in a bribery case involving about RM1 million, would be charged at the Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court on June 18.
He said the individual would face seven charges comprising one count of soliciting a bribe and six counts of receiving bribes under Section 16(a)(B) of the MACC Act 2009.
The charges involve a total of RM450,000 across six transactions.
On April 13, the individual was remanded for five days beginning April 14.
Separately, Abdul Halim said MACC was continuing its investigation into a government contract case involving semiconductor company Arm Holdings and had so far recorded statements from 26 witnesses.
He said the commission was awaiting the outcome of a Mutual Legal Assistance (MLA) application submitted through the Attorney General's Chambers to obtain statements from witnesses overseas.
"Once the process of recording statements from those witnesses is completed, MACC expects to record statements from two Cabinet ministers and one former Cabinet minister to complete the investigation," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MoF flags tender manipulation in govt procurement process
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Finance Ministry (MoF) has detected irregularities in government procurement, including tender specifications being tailored to favour specific vendors, says Treasury secretary-general Tan Sri Johan Mahmood Merican.
He said although procurement was now largely conducted via open tender, there were cases where processes appeared competitive on paper but ultimately left only one qualified bidder after technical and financial evaluations due to skewed specifications.
"Now, almost all procurements are executed via tender. However, as we all know, Malaysians can be quite creative when all tenders are implemented.
"We are seeing many cases where the tender looks good on paper, but after financial and technical evaluations, only one bidder remains because the specifications were tailored towards a single vendor," he said in his speech at the 2026 Malaysian Statutory Bodies Conference here today.
Johan said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, who is also Finance Minister, had halted the practice of direct negotiations for government procurement as part of broader reforms.
He added that the issue had been repeatedly detected across several agencies, with a firm stance being taken to curb such practices.
"I wouldn't mention this if it had only happened once. The MoF tracks these matters, and this is happening across various agencies, including the Finance Ministry itself. The prime minister is taking a strict stand as part of procurement governance reform," he said.
Johan said the Government Procurement Bill 2025 would strengthen accountability and enforcement.
"Previously, procurement issues were treated as administrative matters. If someone had retired, no action could be taken.
"But now, even after retirement, punitive action can still be taken for irregularities. I could also face action if I commit any wrongdoing," he said.
He added that statutory bodies played a key role in driving national development, including in services, innovation, education, investment and social wellbeing.
The conference, themed Statutory Bodies & National Aspirations: Shaping the Future, was organised by the Association of Statutory Bodies of Malaysia in collaboration with the Malaysian Palm Oil Board (MPOB) and Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Powerwell bags RM158.8mil data centre-related contract in Johor
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Powerwell Holdings Bhd's wholly owned subsidiary Kejuruteraan Powerwell Sdn Bhd has clinched a RM158.81 million contract to supply low voltage switchboards in connection with a data centre project in Johor.
The electricity distribution product maker said its unit had received a duly executed supply contract dated June 11 from a leading international contractor for the supply and delivery.
"The supply contract is expected to be fulfilled by the financial year ending March 31, 2027," it said in a filing with Bursa Malaysia today.
Powerwell added that the supply contract is expected to contribute positively to the group's consolidated earnings and net assets for the financial year ending March 31, 2027.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Transport Ministry to discuss e-hailing safety measures after assault cases
Article:
SEPANG: The Transport Ministry will engage e-hailing operators on possible additional safety measures, says Transport Minister Anthony Loke.
He said among the proposals raised was the installation of dashboard cameras in e-hailing vehicles to protect drivers.
However, he said implementation must take into account passenger privacy concerns.
"We will discuss this with e-hailing operators. Some are considering installing cameras such as dashcams to protect drivers, but we need to deliberate further as it also involves passenger privacy, which we must be careful about.
"In terms of safety measures, we will engage further with operators to see what additional steps can be taken to protect drivers.
"Both passenger and driver safety are important, so we need to strike a balance," he said after chairing the Road Safety Council's 62nd annual general meeting here today.
The discussion comes amid recent incidents involving e-hailing drivers being attacked by passengers.
On June 11, police arrested a man and a woman to assist investigations into the
robbery
and assault of an e-hailing driver in Kota Tinggi.
Kota Tinggi police chief Superintendent Yusof Othman said police received a report at 2.46am of a knife-point robbery at the Tun Sri Lanang Bus Terminal.
On June 12, three foreigners were reported to have
assaulted
an e-hailing driver after being reprimanded for making noise inside his vehicle at Taman Tan Sri Yaakob, Skudai.
Iskandar Puteri police chief Asst Comm M. Kumarasan said police were tracking the suspects to assist investigations under Sections 323 and 506 of the Penal Code.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Vantris Energy returns to profit in Q1
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Vantris Energy Bhd returned to the black with net profit of RM145.79 million in the first quarter (Q1) of the financial year ended Jan 31, 2027 (FY27), compared to net loss of RM477.95 million in the same period a year ago.
The oil and gas services firm stated in a Bursa Malaysia filing today that the uplift was primarily attributable to lower loss recognised from a project in Angola as it nears completion, recognition of a favourable insurance claim and reduction in finance cost following the completion of the group's restructuring exercise.
However, revenue fell to RM645.21 million in Q1 FY27, compared to RM801.37 million in Q1 FY26.
"The decline was primarily attributable to lower contributions from the engineering and construction (E&C) segment, reflecting the group's deliberate strategic shift towards contracts with a more balanced risk profile across Southeast Asia and Australia, with a focus on lower-risk, service-oriented activities.
"In addition, there were lower activity levels in the operations and maintenance (O&M) segment due to seasonal factors, but the effects were partially mitigated by stronger performance from the drilling segment, supported by higher contract rates secured," it said.
The E&C segment is expected to remain resilient, supported by the recent completion of several major projects and a deliberate, strategic shift towards contracts with a more balanced risk profile.
With an order book of approximately RM2.3 billion and a healthy pipeline of opportunities currently open for tender, the company said the segment is well-positioned to contribute steadily to the group's future earnings.
"The group will continue to prioritise opportunities within the Southeast Asia and Australia regions while maintaining disciplined project execution, optimising operational efficiencies, and strengthening risk management practices to support the segment's sustainable growth.
"The company's immediate priority is to exit Practice Note 17 status by delivering two consecutive quarters of profitability, sustaining operational momentum, and reinforcing stakeholder confidence. In this regard, the group has achieved profitability in 4Q FY26 and Q1 FY27," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia ends higher, tracking Asian peers on improved global sentiment
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia's key index advanced at Monday's close, tracking gains across regional equities as geopolitical risk sentiment improved following an interim agreement between the United States and Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, said analysts.
At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose by 7.76 points, or 0.46 per cent, to 1,691.39 compared with last Friday's close of 1,683.63.
The key index opened 6.76 points higher at 1,690.39 earlier today and moved between 1,688.04 and 1,697.48 throughout the session.
Market breadth was positive, with gainers outpacing losers 789 to 483, while 467 counters were unchanged, 1,034 untraded and 36 suspended.
Turnover surged to 5.02 billion units worth RM3.91 billion from 2.79 billion units worth RM2.31 billion last Friday.
IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said the US-Iran agreement has reduced near-term concerns over energy supply disruptions and lowered geopolitical risk premiums across global asset markets.
"Although the path towards a comprehensive settlement remains uncertain, the de-escalation has provided a constructive backdrop for risk assets, supporting investor sentiment across emerging markets, including Malaysia," he told Bernama.
Meanwhile, Rakuten Trade Sdn Bhd vice-president of equity research Thong Pak Leng said investors are advised to remain cautious as the peace agreement has yet to be formally signed and geopolitical risks have not been fully eliminated.
That said, easing crude oil prices and improving risk appetite across regional markets should lend support to the local bourse.
"Looking ahead, we maintain a cautiously optimistic view on the market, although investors are likely to remain selective amid lingering geopolitical uncertainties and concerns over the global economic outlook.
"As such, we expect the FBM KLCI to trend within the 1,680-1,700 range for the week," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Wawasan risks splitting opposition vote, weakening bloc – Expert
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Parti Wawasan Negara risks becoming a strategic liability if it ends up competing for the same voter base as other opposition parties instead of expanding its reach, says a political analyst.
Universiti Malaya socio-political analyst Professor Dr Awang Azman Awang Pawi said the danger in Malay politics arose when multiple parties contested the same seats with near-identical narratives centred on Islam, Malay unity, stability and anti-government sentiment.
"At that point, competition no longer expands voter choice but splits the vote within the same pool," he told the New Straits Times.
He said the main beneficiary would be unity government parties, as a fragmented Malay opposition vote could allow government candidates to win with small pluralities.
International Islamic University Malaysia political analyst and ISEAS–Yusof Ishak Institute fellow Associate Professor Dr Syaza Shukri took a more optimistic view, saying Parti Wawasan Negara could reinvigorate the opposition if it drew Bersatu supporters and worked closely with Pas without directly clashing with it.
"Perikatan Nasional (PN) was appearing weak because of the Pas–Bersatu dispute. Now it is clear Parti Wawasan Negara is taking over – PN is back in the game," she said.
University of Tasmania Asia Institute Professor James Chin, however, dismissed concerns over fragmentation, saying the Malay political landscape remained largely divided between Umno, PN and PH.
He said parties without parliamentary representation would eventually fade regardless of pre-election noise.
On what Parti Wawasan Negara could offer that Bersatu could not, analysts were sceptical that any distinction would be meaningful in the near term.
Syaza suggested the party could position itself as a "moderate conservative" platform, while Awang Azman said Bersatu's image had become tied to elitism and internal power struggles despite its original reformist positioning.
He said Parti Wawasan Negara would need fresher Malay leadership not overshadowed by Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin, a grassroots network not overly dependent on Pas, and a stronger economic narrative for urban and middle-class Malays.
On Saturday, Hamzah Zainudin announced the
formation of Parti Wawasan Negara,
saying its name was proposed by Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang.
He said the party's annual general meeting was expected next week, adding that its formation was aimed at consolidating political forces focused on governance reform, national development and the welfare of Malaysians.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Stop blaming Malaysia': Rights group demands Myanmar take accountability for Rohingya crisis
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Rohingya crisis stems from Myanmar's own actions and policies, and responsibility for addressing it cannot be shifted to host countries such as Malaysia, the Centre for Human Rights Research and Advocacy (Centhra) said.
Its chairman, Azril Mohd Amin, proposed a sustainable solution based on three pillars: orderly domestic management, fair international burden-sharing and accountability for Myanmar as the source of the crisis.
On domestic management, Centhra called for the strengthening of the Refugee Documentation Registration Programme and identity verification systems to clearly distinguish between refugees fleeing persecution and undocumented economic migrants.
Azril said enforcement efforts should focus more on human trafficking and smuggling syndicates, while Malaysia should consider developing a specific legal framework for refugee management.
He also proposed limited and conditional work permits for verified refugees to reduce reliance on the informal economy, curb labour exploitation and improve government oversight.
Access to basic education for refugee children should also be prioritised to prevent the emergence of a generation without formal schooling, he said.
On international burden-sharing, Centhra said Malaysia could not continue carrying the humanitarian responsibility alone.
It called on developed countries to increase refugee resettlement quotas more fairly and consistently, and urged Asean, the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) and international partners to consider establishing a joint financing mechanism, such as a Regional Rohingya Fund, to assist countries hosting large refugee populations.
On accountability, Azril said the international community could not continue focusing solely on managing the consequences of the crisis without addressing its root causes.
He urged Asean to play a more proactive role in addressing citizenship issues, systematic discrimination and human rights violations in Myanmar, and said Malaysia could support international efforts to hold those responsible accountable.
"A lasting resolution can only be achieved when the Rohingya are able to return voluntarily, safely, with dignity and sustainably to Myanmar, with full recognition of their citizenship, safety and human rights.
"Malaysia cannot be blamed for the crimes and persecution occurring in Myanmar. But Malaysia also cannot ignore the real challenges faced by its own people," he said.
Centhra noted that the systematic persecution of the Rohingya community had been widely documented by the United Nations and various international human rights mechanisms.
Azril said Malaysia was hosting approximately 215,600 registered refugees and asylum seekers as of February 2026, including 193,824 from Myanmar, of whom about 126,144 were Rohingya.
Centhra acknowledged that although Malaysia is not a signatory to the 1951 Refugee Convention, it has allowed most Rohingya refugees to live within local communities, work informally and access certain humanitarian assistance and basic services.
Azril said the government had both the right and responsibility to enforce immigration laws and protect national interests, but that such enforcement must be carried out in line with human rights principles.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah records 5.9pct drop in drug addiction cases, says Hajiji
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: Sabah recorded a 5.9 per cent drop in drug addiction cases, says Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor.
He said there were 8,665 cases last year compared with 9,607 in 2024.
Sabah also recorded the lowest rate in the country last year, with 230 drug addicts and substance abusers detected.
"This demonstrates the effectiveness of coordinated efforts by all parties in curbing both the supply and demand of drugs in Sabah," he said after chairing the State Drug Eradication Action Council meeting at Menara Kinabalu here.
Hajiji also commended the National Anti-Drugs Agency for its community outreach efforts through grassroots prevention programmes.
"I am grateful for the agency's commitment in reaching out to the community on Banggi Island in Kudat recently through the Drug-Free Aspirations Roadshow in collaboration with the Two-Wheel Anti-Drug Squad programme," he said.
He added that police had also played their part in drug seizures.
Among matters discussed at the meeting were the expansion of drug-free workplace programmes in the public sector and the strengthening of the Sabah State Anti-Drug Squad.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Loke: Safe School Zone pilot rolled out at 14 schools nationwide
Article:
SEPANG: The Road Safety Council is rolling out a Safe School Zone pilot project at 14 schools nationwide, benefiting nearly 20,000 pupils and school staff.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the initiative was introduced following several recent incidents involving the deaths of schoolchildren.
"Statistics also show that, on average, nearly 70 accidents occur in school zones each year.
"This indicates that speed management measures and road infrastructure around many schools are still not at a satisfactory level," he said in his speech at the Road Safety Council's 62nd annual general meeting here today.
Loke said the project focused on reducing vehicle speeds in school zones through infrastructure improvements, including signalised pedestrian crossings, road narrowing measures and pedestrian walkways.
He said a Safe School Zone project in Brickfields had already been completed and would serve as a model for wider implementation.
He added that the council planned to expand the initiative to more high-risk schools, particularly those along busy roads and federal routes.
Loke said this required cooperation from all parties, especially the Education Ministry and state education departments, to identify schools facing the highest risks.
"There are schools at particularly high risk due to the large number of heavy vehicles passing through the area, particularly those located along roadside or federal routes.
"These are the schools that will be targeted for Safe School Zones, where interventions will be implemented," he said.
Loke also called on the corporate and private sectors to support the initiative to allow more schools to benefit.
"Any corporate or private company can sponsor a Safe School Zone, perhaps with their branding displayed within the school area.
"For instance, a company could adopt five or 10 schools each year. If this approach is implemented, the project can be scaled up," he said.
He added the project cost between RM200,000 and RM300,000 per school, covering road furniture and related infrastructure, including speed bumps.
Separately, Loke said the council would develop a road safety index to monitor compliance with traffic regulations and changes in road user behaviour at high-risk locations.
"As we strengthen road safety interventions, our capacity to make data-driven decisions must also be enhanced.
"Currently, performance measurement focuses largely on fatality figures, which are lagging indicators.
"We need a more dynamic approach through leading indicators to identify risk levels, system vulnerabilities and the effectiveness of interventions," he said.
Loke also announced an allocation of RM30,000 for each state road safety council to support operational needs and strengthen road safety programmes at state and community levels.
He said the council must be strengthened in terms of structure and operational capacity to play a more effective strategic role.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: GLIC investment commitment in Bumiputera firms increased to RM2bil this year
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Investment commitments by government-linked investment companies (GLICs) in Bumiputera businesses have been increased to RM2 billion for 2026, compared with RM1.3 billion last year, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
In a post on social media platform X, he said the commitment includes the Bumiputera Relay Initiative (Larian Ganti Bumiputera), which aims to expand access to financing and investment for Bumiputera entrepreneurs at every stage of business growth.
Anwar chaired the Second Meeting of the Bumiputera Economic Council (MEB) for 2026 today.
He said the move continues efforts to strengthen Bumiputera institutions, which began with the consolidation of the government-linked private equity company EKUINAS under the Bumiputera Investment Foundation (Yayasan Pelaburan Bumiputra).
Anwar also said that to strengthen monitoring of Bumiputera development programmes, particularly initiatives under PuTERA35, the Bumiputera Agenda Steering Unit (TERAJU), currently overseen by the Economy Ministry, will be transferred to the Prime Minister's Department (JPM) as an agency.
"To date, the overall achievement of PuTERA35 has reached 64.6 per cent, with 160 out of 493 planned programmes successfully implemented.
"This progress reflects the Madani Government's continued commitment to ensuring that the Bumiputera development agenda is translated into tangible outcomes that directly benefit the people," he added.
Anwar said the meeting was also informed that GLICs and government-linked companies (GLCs) spent RM49.2 billion last year, particularly through procurement from Bumiputera vendors and suppliers.
At the same time, he said Khazanah Nasional Bhd will launch the Dana Ciptawan Fund with an allocation of RM200 million to support Bumiputera mid-tier companies, thereby strengthening their capacity to grow, compete and penetrate wider markets.
In addition, the meeting reviewed the resolutions of the 2026 Bumiputera Education Congress, which was held on May 1.
Further consideration will be given to several strategic proposals, including the establishment of a National Tahfiz Council, to ensure that efforts to strengthen Bumiputera education and human capital development are implemented in a more comprehensive and effective manner.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: New RM650mil system to revolutionise Malaysia's healthcare delivery
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: A new RM650 million nationwide system is set to revolutionise healthcare services at government facilities, promising shorter waiting times, better care and seamless access to records for patients.
Launched today, the National Digital Health Ecosystem and Connectivity Catalyst Initiative (Persada) will use a Cloud-Based Clinical Management System (CCMS) to enhance operational efficiency, improve doctor-patient care and streamline Malaysia's telehealth development.
Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said this will put an end to the era of manual records and paper-based legacy systems, adding that digitalisation is in fact, "the backbone of the continued reform and sustainability of the nation's healthcare system."
With Persada, the ministry is targeting 81 per cent of patients to receive treatment within an hour, said Dzulkefly.
"We have been moving quite aggressively toward the 'One individual, one health aspiration'," he said, adding that the system would enable healthcare facilities to handle an additional capacity of between 75,000 and 300,000 patients every hour.
A memorandum of understanding was signed between Dzulkefly and Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil at the Health Ministry headquarters today, formalising a joint rollout of the initiative by the two ministries.
The project will be implemented in two phases up to 2028, covering 150 government hospitals and 2,488 public healthcare facilities nationwide.
At its core, a system such as Persada and its CCMS platform—supported by the Health Ministry and the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) —is expected to improve patient care by allowing doctors and nurses to access medical histories in real time.
This shift away from paper files and fragmented transfers may reduce waiting times, speed up diagnosis and minimise repeated tests if implemented effectively.
Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the first phase of the project is estimated to cost RM74 million, with the total programme cost reaching RM650 million.
"Right now, the funds will be managed through the Universal Service Provision Fund (USP Fund) under the Ministry of Communications and the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC)," he said.
He added that the system is designed to ensure full interoperability across Malaysia's healthcare ecosystem.
"This will enable all of our facilities — not only those under the Health Ministry, but also public and private hospitals and clinics — to achieve interoperability," Dzulkefly said.
The Persada initiative also expands telehealth capabilities by integrating the Malaysia Virtual Health Assistant System (MyVAS) and the National Information Dissemination Centre (Nadi) aimed at widening healthcare access, particularly in rural areas.
"We have many National Information Dissemination Centres, or Nadi, and these Nadi centres often collaborate with health clinics to serve as reference points for patients," Fahmi said.
Nadi, a community internet access platform, recorded 1.4 million participants in February.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 2026 BYD Atto 3 gets over 1,000 bookings in 10 days
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: BYD Sime Motors has secured more than 1,000 bookings for the newly launched 2026 BYD ATTO 3 within just 10 days of its introduction.
In a statement, BYD Sime Motors said the achievement mirrors the model's Malaysian debut in 2022, when the BYD ATTO 3 reached the same booking milestone within an identical timeframe.
It noted that the early booking momentum underscores continued interest in electric vehicles (EVs) among Malaysians and reflects the growing appeal of models that deliver practicality, innovation and value in a rapidly evolving automotive landscape.
"The response also builds on the strong track record of the BYD ATTO 3 in Malaysia, where it remains the nation's all-time best-selling EV model with more than 12,000 units delivered to date.
"This has solidified BYD's position as the country's leading EV brand," it said.
BYD Malaysia managing director Jacob Ma said the overwhelming response reflected the changing expectations of Malaysian motorists.
"Today's drivers are increasingly seeking vehicles that seamlessly combine technology, convenience and everyday usability, and the 2026 BYD ATTO 3 delivers on this.
"This strong start reaffirms the BYD ATTO 3's position as a favourite in Malaysia's EV landscape," he said.
Supporting its expansion, BYD Sime Motors currently operates a nationwide network of 44 sales and marketing outlets, 25 aftersales facilities and 12 body and paint centres.
Backed by certified technicians, a premium ownership experience, and a 95 per cent parts fill rate, the company continues to enhance service excellence while supporting Malaysia's transition towards electric mobility.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Perodua slashes QV-E prices by nearly RM20,000, thanks to higher localisation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Higher localisation levels have enabled Perusahaan Otomobil Kedua Sdn Bhd (Perodua) to lower the price of its first battery electric vehicle (BEV), the QV-E.
It also helps the country's largest carmake strengthen local supply chains and support domestic jobs.
The QV-E is now priced at RM93,999 for outright ownership, or RM69,999 under the Battery as a Service (BaaS) programme, which comes with a monthly fee of RM215 inclusive of tax, for nine years.
To help consumers manage rising living costs, Perodua is also offering a limited-time promotional price until Sept 30.
Under the offer, the QV-E can be purchased outright for RM87,499, or for RM63,499 with the BaaS package, while the monthly BaaS fee remains unchanged at RM215.
The BaaS package includes battery protection, insurance, replacement and disposal services throughout the leasing period, giving customers peace of mind over battery ownership and maintenance.
At the launch last December, the QV-E was originally introduced with a retail price of RM80,000 for the vehicle body, which excluded insurance and the battery pack.
To keep the entry price low, Perodua introduced a BaaS monthly leasing scheme, which was originally around RM297/month (including sales and service tax) on a nine-year contract.
Perodua said greater use of locally manufactured components has improved production efficiency, reduced dependence on imported parts and increased supply chain resilience.
"Perodua and its local vendors have been able to manufacture a significant number of parts locally, and this means that we can further protect local jobs while be less dependent on foreign technology and supply chains," Perodua president and chief executive officer Datuk Seri Zainal Abidin Ahmad said.
He said the localisation drive has also helped build a more reliable inventory of spare parts, particularly body components, ensuring faster availability for customers.
"This development is important as we can now build a more resilient inventory of spare parts – especially the car body parts – to ensure that customers can rely on readily available domestically produced items when they need it," he said.
Perodua has also increased QV-E production to more than 500 units a month, allowing the company to further optimise operations and pass cost savings on to consumers.
"Due to localisation, we are able to further optimise our operations, and we are now sharing those benefits with our valued customers," Zainal said.
"For those who wants to buy the QV-E on outright purchase, Perodua does offer to buy the battery when comes the time to dispose of it (the battery)," Zainal said.
The QV-E, which carries a five-star Asean NCAP safety rating, is equipped with a 52.5kWh lithium iron phosphate battery and offers a range of up to 445km under the NEDC cycle, depending on driving style and road conditions.
Existing QV-E customers will receive a special refund and will see their monthly BaaS payment reduced to RM215 from RM275 previously, effective from their next payment cycle.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Foreign investors buy China's onshore yuan bonds in May, first time in over a year
Article:
SHANGHAI: Foreign investors bought Chinese onshore yuan-denominated bonds in May for the first time since April 2025, official data showed on Monday.
Foreign institutions held 3.21 trillion yuan (US$475 billion) in bonds traded on China's interbank market as of the end of May, the central bank's Shanghai head office said, up from 3.12 trillion yuan a month earlier.
China's bond market has been insulated from a global rout that has driven benchmark yields up by between 35 and 60 basis points in the United States, Britain, the euro zone and Japan since the outbreak of the Iran conflict.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Turkish-built command platform to replace Sabah's frontline sea base
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Tun Sharifah Rodziah Sea Base, which served as a frontline bastion safeguarding the security and sovereignty of the nation's waters in Eastern Sabah for nearly a decade, will be replaced by a Turkish-built Multi-Purpose Command Platform (MPCP).
The National Security Council (NSC) said procurement of the MPCP was approved under the 13th Malaysia Plan and would be carried out through government-to-government (G2G) cooperation between Malaysia and Türkiye.
"The MoU will be signed between National Security director-general YM Raja Datuk Nushirwan Zainal Abidin and Mustafa M. Şeker, vice-president of systems and platforms at Türkiye's Secretariat of Defence Industries (SSB)," it said in a statement.
The NSC described the MPCP as a game-changing asset that would significantly enhance surveillance capabilities along Eastern Sabah's maritime border.
Serving as a versatile forward operating base, the platform will be equipped with long-range surveillance radar and unmanned systems, including unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) and unmanned surface vehicles (USVs).
"The vessel will possess the dynamic capability to conduct a wide range of maritime enforcement operations in an aggressive and effective manner.
"As a platform that can be deployed flexibly to security hotspots, its presence is expected to significantly reduce security threats in Eastern Sabah," the statement said.
The NSC said replacing the sea base with an MPCP vessel would provide greater mobility, more advanced technology, and more efficient operations than the previous static platform.
It said Türkiye was selected as a strategic partner due to the maturity and rapid advancement of its defence industry.
"The G2G cooperation will not only ensure transparency in the procurement process but will also serve as a catalyst for a mutually beneficial long-term security relationship, encompassing technology transfer, investment in facilities in Malaysia and the exchange of expertise between the two countries."
The Tun Sharifah Rodziah Sea Base, located off Semporna, was
officially decommissioned on June 10
after serving as a forward operating base since July 31, 2015.
Its functions and role are currently being assumed by Auxiliary Vessel Tun Azizan (Kataz) to ensure the continuity of the Royal Malaysian Navy's operational presence and readiness in Eastern Sabah.
Originally a mobile offshore production unit known as Hercules 259 built in the United States, the platform was transformed into a forward sea base through a strategic collaboration involving the Royal Malaysian Navy, Petronas and Sapura Kencana.
The NSC said the MPCP would further strengthen the preparedness, monitoring and rapid response capabilities of enforcement agencies against threats such as intrusions, smuggling and cross-border crime.
"Drawing on its extensive experience operating in Sabah waters, the navy will lead the operation of the MPCP.
"This robust deterrence approach is critical not only for defending national sovereignty but also for ensuring the security of Eastern Sabah waters and protecting maritime economic activities in the area."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: REV bags seven awards at MDA d'Awards 2026
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: REV Media Group (REV) secured seven awards at the Malaysian Digital Association's (MDA) d'Awards 2026, including Gold awards for Publisher of the Year and Content Agency of the Year.
The company received three Gold and four Silver awards across categories spanning digital publishing, branded content, artificial intelligence (AI)-driven innovation, audience growth, integrated screens and talent development.
REV's Gold wins for Publisher of the Year and Content Agency of the Year recognised its audience-first content strategy, multilingual editorial capabilities, platform expertise and culturally relevant storytelling.
The company also won Gold for Best Digital Growth Campaign, reflecting its efforts in audience expansion and digital platform performance.
In addition, REV received Silver awards for Best AI & Digital Tech Products, Best Use of Content/Advertorial/Native Advertising, Best Use of Integrated Screens and Rising Star.
REV Media Group chief executive officer, Samuel Wee, said the awards reflected the breadth of the company's capabilities across publishing, branded content, AI products, platform growth and integrated campaign solutions.
"These wins are meaningful because they reflect the full range of what REV is building, from publishing and branded content to AI products, platform growth and integrated campaign solutions.
"At the heart of it, our focus remains the same: understanding Malaysian audiences, creating work that earns attention and using technology with purpose.
"This is a team achievement, and I am proud of everyone across REV who continues to raise the standard for digital media in Malaysia," he said.
The awards also recognised the company's work in AI innovation, brand storytelling, campaign execution and talent development.
Beyond its publishing operations, REV continues to support Malaysia's digital ecosystem through industry collaborations, platform innovation and youth outreach programmes.
Among these initiatives is Tech For Good Youth, which provides students with practical exposure to content creation, media and technology.
The latest recognition reinforces REV's position in Malaysia's digital publishing industry, with a continued focus on publishing excellence, responsible AI adoption, audience growth and industry development.
REV manages more than 30 digital brands across Malay, English and Chinese-language platforms, reaching more than 16 million unique visitors monthly, according to the company.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Urban poor not sidelined, Bukit Kiara project proves rights upheld
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government has never sidelined the rights of the urban poor in their pursuit of a better life, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
He said the Bukit Kiara longhouse permanent housing project was proof that the rights of urban poor communities could be upheld, even after more than four decades of waiting for proper homes.
"This project is not big, but its historical impact is significant. In many parts of the world, urban poor communities living in longhouses under housing pressure, surrounded by mansions and facing environmental constraints, have rarely prevailed.
"Alhamdulillah, here you have succeeded because you defended your rights and followed the law," he said in his speech at the groundbreaking ceremony here today.
Also present were Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh and Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan.
The project involves the construction of 200 apartment units in an 18-storey block, with each original homeowner to receive two units. Each unit will have three bedrooms, and construction is scheduled to begin today and is expected to be completed within three years.
Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, also announced a RM1 million grant to subsidise maintenance costs for residents for three years after completion. The allocation will be channelled to Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL).
He said resolving the long-standing issue was not straightforward as it involved a lengthy legal process that had to be respected.
He added that the case should not be misrepresented as a precedent for redevelopment of squatter settlements without due process.
"This followed the legal process, and the judges carefully examined all aspects and heard all views. That is why I say this is historically important. We must protect the rights of the poor, regardless of whether they are Malay, Chinese or Indian," he said.
Meanwhile, Yeoh said the 44-year wait by Bukit Kiara longhouse residents had entered its resolution phase with the start of the project.
She said the settlement was based on key principles, including limiting development to 5.76 acres, returning the remaining land to the government to be gazetted as public open space, and ensuring replacement homes are built first so residents can move directly into their new homes without temporary relocation.
Yeoh said a joint audit involving Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL), Friends of Bukit Kiara and the Forest Research Institute Malaysia would be carried out to ensure proper management and protection of trees at the site.
She said an existing temple site would be retained and officially zoned as a non-Muslim place of worship, while parking bays for Taman Rimba Kiara would be increased from 80 to 150.
"This development shows that housing needs and environmental preservation can go hand in hand through an approach that prioritises public interest and community wellbeing," she added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bersatu blasts Samsuri for 'very irresponsible' leadership in PN crisis
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz has accused Perikatan Nasional (PN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar of failing to act in his capacity as coalition chief, saying he appeared to have forgotten that he was coalition chairman and not Pas chairman.
Tun Faisal said Samsuri had yet to call a PN supreme council meeting to discuss preparations for the Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections, negotiate seat allocations among component parties, or convene a committee to discuss an electoral pact with parties outside the coalition.
"Very irresponsible," he said to the New Straits Times today.
He said Bersatu remained in PN, but stressed that Pas' decision to sever ties with Bersatu at a time when the coalition needed to prepare for two imminent state elections gave the impression that Pas was not prioritising PN's interests and was willing to act against them.
He said Pas' failure to resolve the coalition's internal issues had created uncertainty among members, supporters and the election machinery.
Tun Faisal said the admission by Johor Pas commissioner Datuk Dr Mahfodz Mohamed that the party's decision to cut ties with Bersatu had affected preparations for the Johor state election - and that Johor Pas intended to use its own logo - was proof that Pas' actions were irresponsible and damaging to PN's election readiness.
The remarks come after Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said Datuk
Hamzah Zainudin
will be reappointed as opposition leader and said Pas would use the PN logo in the upcoming state elections.
Datuk Dr Marzuki Mohamad, a former aide to Muhyiddin, later accused Hadi of acting like the "supreme leader" of PN, saying Hamzah's reappointment was not discussed at a PN Supreme Council meeting.
He added that the appointment contradicted a previous PN decision on May 16 naming Samsuri to the post.
Last Monday, Hadi said Pas central committee had decided to
end political cooperation
with Bersatu and explore forming a new political pact ahead of the next elections.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Anwar: People suffer when politicians stir racial tensions
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged Malaysians not to be swayed by campaigns aimed at dividing communities along racial lines.
He said such efforts undermine national unity and ultimately harm ordinary people, whose concerns are often overshadowed by divisive politics.
"That is why I always appeal to the people not to listen to campaigns that divide us by saying this is Malay, this is Chinese, and therefore we must fight. The ones who lose are the people below.
"Their problems are wide-ranging and long-standing. Who will champion them if everyone is fighting over race?" he said at the groundbreaking ceremony for the Bukit Kiara longhouse permanent housing project here today.
Also present were Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh and Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan.
Anwar said Malaysia could become a fairer and more prosperous nation if policies were clear, leadership remained committed to reform, and Malaysians rejected divisive politics.
He said the country must work towards a more just society that safeguards the wellbeing of all communities.
"Under Madani, values such as humanity, compassion and ihsan are important principles that we must continue to uphold.
"Let us move forward together – I see Indians, Malays and Chinese here – to care for one another as human beings and preserve what is good in us," he said.
On Saturday, Anwar said racial and religious
hatred
should be countered with patience, wisdom and good conduct, rather than anger.
He said although Malaysia was often cited as a model of multicultural harmony, it continued to face challenges from racial rhetoric, religious hostility and divisive narratives.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chow: Penang PH wants more women candidates, but still a challenge
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Penang Pakatan Harapan (PH) hopes to field more women candidates in the next state election, although identifying enough suitable and willing candidates remains a challenge, says its chairman Chow Kon Yeow.
He said Penang PH remained committed to achieving at least 30 per cent women's participation in politics and decision-making, but continued to face difficulties in recruiting sufficient candidates.
"That is certainly our aspiration. In every election, we will field women candidates, but the actual number depends on whether there are enough suitable and willing individuals.
"While we aspire to have more women candidates, there must first be enough for us to choose from. That is an issue we need to address," he told reporters after officiating the World Women Economic and Business Summit 2026 here today.
Chow, who is also Penang Chief Minister, said that although women had made significant progress in education, business, engineering and the public sector, their participation in politics still faced distinct challenges.
He said Penang PH had been proactive in supporting the 30 per cent target, but relatively few women were willing to step forward during candidate selection due to the pressures of political life.
Earlier in his speech, Chow said Malaysia's 30 per cent women's representation target in politics, introduced in 2009, had yet to be achieved, with women making up only 13.5 per cent of MPs and 12 per cent of state assembly members.
He said the gap called for renewed commitment and concrete action, adding that political parties should consider institutionalising the target in their candidate selection processes.
Chow also said parties should ensure equal representation of women on decision-making committees, while strengthening access to resources and mentoring for emerging women leaders.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UK PM announces ban on social media for under-16s
Article:
LONDON: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Monday announced a forthcoming total ban on social media for children under 16, saying that such platforms are "making children unhappy."
Starmer said that the government "will ban access to social media for all children under the age of 16", warning that these platforms are "exposing them to content that is dangerous" and "designed to be addictive."
The government said the ban will "include platforms like Snapchat, TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook and X" but not messaging services such as WhatsApp.
He added he hoped to pass the regulation by late December and for the ban to come into force in spring next year.
The prime minister also said the government would go further and take "world-leading action on gaming services and live streaming platforms."
The government said in a statement it would also be considering overnight curfews and breaks in infinite scrolling for under 18s, and would announce more detail in July.
Starmer said the upcoming ban was influenced by the experience of Australia, which in December became the first nation to ban people under 16 from social media.
The announcement follows a government-led consultation where British teenagers trialled social media bans and time limits on apps.
A spokesperson for YouTube responded with a warning that such a blanket ban would push children towards "less safe services."
Starmer said that the government was "taking action" on gaming services and live streaming platforms that allow strangers to contact children.
"Is there a situation in the offline world where you would just let your child pair up with a stranger? An adult that you don't know about? No. So we're taking action on that," he said, without giving details.
Canada's culture minister last week introduced legislation that would ban children under 16 from having social media accounts and require AI chatbot services to limit production of harmful content.
The proposed Digital Safety Act makes Canada the latest in a number of countries cracking down on social media platforms over concerns of harm to children.
Indonesia began enforcing its own social media ban for users under the age of 16 in March, while several European governments have announced their desire to make similar moves.
'MORAL RESPONSIBILITY'
The UK government's consultation on the issue, which closed in late May, attracted about 116,000 contributions, making it the second-largest response ever received.
Over 83 percent of parents who responded said the risks posed by social media outweighed the benefits for children, with 91 percent backing a minimum age of 16.
The UK announcement comes a week after the government said tech giants must stop children in Britain from being able to send and receive nude images on their devices.
Britain's interior ministry said it was giving companies including Apple and Google three months to introduce safety features to block children from taking and accessing naked photos on phones and tablets.
If they failed to do so, the government would introduce legislation forcing them to activate the technology, it warned.
Starmer's centre-left Labour government said technology companies had a "moral responsibility" to "protect children from coercion, abuse and sextortion."
A law change would stop children from being able to access pornography, while also making it more difficult for child abusers to target children, it said.
According to analysis by the Internet Watch Foundation charity cited by the government, 91 percent of online child sexual abuse reports recorded in 2024 contained self-generated content from children themselves. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Selangor finances stay strong amid global headwinds, says MB
Article:
SHAH ALAM: Selangor's financial position remains healthy despite global economic uncertainties arising from supply disruptions and fluctuations in world markets.
Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the state government saw no need for emergency measures as revenue collection continued on a positive trajectory.
He said Selangor had collected RM1.6 billion as of May against a RM2.8 billion target for this year, with current trends showing steady progress.
"We have not pressed the panic button, but we are monitoring developments on a weekly and monthly basis," he said at a press conference after launching the Selangor Resilience Strengthening Package Phase 1 at the State Secretary's Office building here today.
Amirudin said the state also recorded a RM300 million surplus against expenditure due to prudent spending.
While acknowledging that this year's financial position was not as strong as last year's, he said Selangor remained confident of meeting its revenue target and maintained healthy reserves.
He said efforts were ongoing to strengthen revenue streams, including land disposal exercises, rezoning of industrial land, and new investment inflows into the state.
Amirudin added that the full impact of global economic uncertainty was expected to emerge in the coming months, but the state government remained proactive in mitigating risks to the people and the economy.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Op Tiris: GOF deployment deters petrol, diesel smuggling to Thailand
Article:
KOTA BARU: The deployment of General Operations Force (GOF) personnel at petrol stations identified as smuggling hotspots along the Kelantan–Thailand border has deterred attempts to smuggle subsidised petrol and diesel across the border, said GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain.
He said the deployment, carried out under Op Tiris, had forced smugglers to alter their modus operandi as their movements were being closely monitored by GOF personnel stationed at the premises.
"The deployment of GOF personnel at these stations, particularly in high-risk areas from Tumpat to Jeli, has made it increasingly difficult for smugglers to carry out their activities.
"The constant monitoring has forced syndicates to change their methods and movements to avoid detection," he said.
He said random inspections conducted on vehicles at all nine petrol stations had had a significant impact on the movement of vehicles believed to be involved in fuel smuggling activities.
He said the inspections had curtailed smugglers' ability to obtain subsidised fuel freely and transport it across the border.
"Throughout the operation, we have not detected any vehicles fitted with modified fuel tanks at the stations involved."
Radzi said GOF had also refined its operational strategy by focusing on vehicles that repeatedly refuel at specific petrol stations.
He said this approach enabled personnel to identify suspicious patterns and restrict attempts by syndicates to obtain large quantities of subsidised petrol and diesel.
"The strategy has made it more difficult for smugglers to secure fuel supplies on a large scale and has limited their ability to operate openly," he said.
He added that the visible presence of GOF personnel at the petrol stations had created a strong deterrent effect, reducing the movement of smuggling vehicles in border areas.
He said the communities had also expressed confidence in and support for the operation, believing it would help prevent subsidised fuel from being smuggled out of the country.
"The public's support is important because it strengthens our efforts to curb fuel smuggling and protect government subsidies intended for Malaysians," he said.
Intelligence gathered by GOF, he added, indicated that some operators of illegal jetties and smuggling bases had become increasingly concerned as obtaining subsidised fuel supplies had become more challenging.
GOF began deploying personnel at
nine petrol stations
along the Malaysia–Thailand border identified as high-risk for subsidised fuel leakages in April this year.
Four of the stations are in Tumpat, two in Tanah Merah, two in Pasir Mas and one in Jeli.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Shares and bonds surge, oil slides on Iran deal
Article:
LONDON/SYDNEY: Share markets and bonds rallied hard on Monday and oil prices tumbled five per cent as a framework peace deal between the United States and Iran promised to ease inflationary pressures globally and lessen the need for higher interest rates.
Europe's early optimism drove both the STOXX 600 and FTSE Eurofirst to records. Asia's biggest markets had all leapt overnight, while futures markets pointed to gains of 1.3 per cent to 2.0 per cent for Wall Street later.
Early on Monday, Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said on social media an Iranian peace deal had been struck, while President Donald Trump said the pact included opening the vital Strait of Hormuz, though without giving details.
Trump will meet Middle Eastern leaders and attend a working session with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy during a G7 summit in France this week.
Iran said traffic through the strait would be regulated by it and Oman, a potential blow to the rules of free trade that suggests there might be a toll of some kind on transits.
Saxo Bank strategist John Hardy said the peace deal was "about as supportive as you can get" for markets, especially after the excitement triggered by the recent record-breaking US$75 billion SpaceX IPO.
"What do you add on from here to get sentiment even bubblier?"
The news will be a relief for the crowd of central banks meeting this week, easing some pressure to tighten policy to head off an energy-driven rise in inflationary expectations.
Markets had already priced in a likely deal but the confirmation was enough to send down Brent crude 5.0 per cent, to stand at US$83 a barrel, well off its May peak of US$126.41, albeit still above the US$67 where it traded before the war began in late February.
"We see Brent oil futures falling to US$80 by the end of the year, assuming the strait does not close again," said Vivek Dhar, a mining and energy analyst at CBA.
"Our forecast implicitly assumes that oil and refined product exports can resume quickly through the Strait of Hormuz, but this view carries considerable uncertainty tied to the damage to oil and refinery assets."
The prospect of cheaper oil will be a boon to Japan which is a net importer of energy.
Overnight the Nikkei had surged 5.0 per cent. South Korea's red-hot market gained 5.2 per cent, and Chinese blue chips firmed 1.4 per cent. MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan rose 2.4 per cent.
RELIEF FOR CENTRAL BANKS
Central banks are set to meet in the United States, Britain, Japan, Australia, Switzerland, Sweden, Norway and Russia this week, with Japan considered likely to lift rates this time.
The Federal Reserve is widely expected to leave rates at 3.50 per cent to 3.75 per cent on Wednesday at Chair Kevin Warsh's debut meeting. The statement, economic projections and news conference will be scrutinised for any signs of the Fed dropping its easing bias as officials grow more hawkish on inflation risks.
Saxo Bank's Hardy said the change of hands was set to be the biggest at the world's most influential central bank since Ben Bernanke succeeded Alan Greenspan in early 2006.
"The whole communication strategy is going to be completely different under Warsh," he said. "They are going to hold their cards a lot closer to their chest."
Investors were quick to trim the chance of a Fed hike this year, with December futures edging up four ticks while a move as early as October is now priced around 30 per cent.
Treasuries rallied on hopes that oil prices would now fall sustainably and lessen the upside risks for inflation.
Yields on 2-year notes dropped six basis points to 4.02 per cent. The equivalent two-year German yield, sensitive to European Central Bank interest rate expectations, also fell four basis points to a two-week low of 2.571 per cent.
The drop in yields and general improvement in risk pulled the US dollar broadly lower, with the euro rising 0.4 per cent to US$1.1617, while sterling rose 0.3 per cent to US$1.3446.
The dollar fared slightly better on the yen at 160.00 , which is stuck in a bear trend even though the Bank of Japan is expected to raise rates by 25 basis points to 1 per cent t on Tuesday.
The Bank of England is expected to hold rates at 3.75 per cent on Thursday and through 2026, with policymakers seen in no rush to tighten. The BoE's vote split and monetary policy report will be of interest.
Top-tier British data includes May inflation and retail sales, and April employment. Thursday's Makerfield election will also be watched, as a win for Labour Mayor Andy Burnham could set up a leadership contest with Prime Minister Keir Starmer.
In commodity markets, the drop in yields helped non-interest-paying gold climb 2.5 per cent to US$4,322 an ounce, while in the cryptomarkets bitcoin was also 2.5 per cent higher, at US$65,633.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain to hit KL,  nine states
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds for nine states and the Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur until 6pm today.
It said in a statement that, apart from the whole of Perlis, the adverse weather conditions are also expected to hit Kedah (Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar and Pokok Sena); Terengganu (Besut, Setiu, Kuala Nerus, Hulu Terengganu, Kuala Terengganu, Marang and Dungun); and Pahang (Raub, Bentong, Temerloh, Bera and Rompin).
Similar weather conditions are also expected in Selangor (Hulu Selangor, Gombak and Hulu Langat); Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu, Kuala Pilah, Jempol and Tampin); and Johor (Segamat, Batu Pahat, Kluang, Mersing, Pontian and Kulai).
In Sarawak, the areas predicted to be affected are Sri Aman (Sri Aman), Betong, Sarikei, Sibu (Sibu and Selangau), Mukah, Bintulu (Tatau and Bintulu), Miri (Subis and Marudi) and Limbang.
In Sabah, warnings have been issued for the Interior (Keningau and Tambunan), the West Coast, Tawau (Tawau, Kunak and Lahad Datu) and Sandakan (Sandakan).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two suspected scam operatives nabbed with multiple phones, SIM cards near border
Article:
RANTAU PANJANG: Two Chinese nationals suspected of involvement in online scam activities were detained near the Malaysia-Thailand border after they were found in possession of dozens of mobile phones and foreign SIM cards.
General Operations Force (GOF) Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the men, aged 32 and 34, were arrested in Kampung Siram near Jeram Perdah here early today.
"Acting on public information, GOF personnel from the Eighth Battalion conducted checks in a bushy area at about 4.30am and spotted two men behaving suspiciously.
"On inspection, both failed to produce valid travel documents and are believed to have entered the country illegally via an unauthorised jetty earlier the same morning. They were detained for illegal entry," he said.
Radzi said a further search uncovered 45 mobile phones, 142 foreign SIM cards and two pen drives, with the items valued at about RM48,000.
"Preliminary findings suggest possible links to online scam activities, based on the large number of phones and SIM cards in their possession.
"However, further questioning could not be carried out as both suspects were unable to communicate in Bahasa Malaysia or English," he said.
He said the seized items and suspects had been handed over to the Pasir Mas district police for further action.
The case is being investigated under Section 6(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 for illegal entry into Malaysia.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: IGP: 10,000 CCTV cameras will boost enforcement efforts in KL
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The installation of 10,000 closed-circuit television (CCTV) cameras across Kuala Lumpur will strengthen policing and assist law enforcement efforts.
Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail said the expanded use of surveillance technology would help police overcome manpower limitations while improving operational efficiency.
"It is a very good move because I believe CCTV and camera technology can assist us.
"The manpower within the force is not unlimited, but with technological advancements and technological support, I believe we can better identify suspects and support our operations," he said.
Khalid said technology, including information technology and artificial intelligence, should be further enhanced within the force to improve investigations and daily policing duties.
"That is why I have always encouraged the use of technology, IT and AI within the force.
"These capabilities need to be strengthened and improved because they can indirectly help us solve investigations and carry out our duties more effectively," he said.
He was speaking after launching the "Combat Scam: Two Teams, One Goal" campaign in collaboration with Public Bank.
Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) had allocated
RM500 million for the installation
of 10,000 CCTV cameras around Kuala Lumpur as part of efforts to enhance public safety and urban monitoring.
Khalid expressed confidence that greater adoption of technology would complement conventional policing methods and contribute to a safer environment for the public.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Special tribunal proposed to investigate alleged Rohingya smuggling network
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Persatuan Melayu Pulau Pinang (Pemenang) has urged the government to establish a special tribunal to investigate allegations of a syndicate smuggling Rohingya refugees into Malaysia.
Its president, Tan Sri Dr Mohd Yussof Latiff, said the alleged syndicate was not only exploiting vulnerable refugees but also eroding the country's dignity, security and sovereignty.
He claimed the smuggling activities were carried out systematically through networks involving local agents and foreigners, with refugees transported by sea from camps in neighbouring countries before entering Malaysia.
He cited allegations raised by lawyer Nurul Hafizah Hassan, who reportedly exposed the existence of organised smuggling operations involving substantial payments and alleged bribery.
He alleged that women and underage girls were among those being brought into the country illegally, purportedly to be married to Rohingya men already residing in Malaysia.
"The activities have led to the continued growth of the Rohingya population in Malaysia through illegal means, while criminal syndicates reap significant profits from the exploitation of refugees," he said in a statement today.
He said the issue of Rohingya settlements in Malaysia had reached a critical stage, citing concerns over illegal settlements and increasing enforcement operations by the authorities.
He said Pemenang was concerned that failure to address the alleged syndicates would worsen social, security and legal challenges linked to undocumented migrants.
"As such, we call for a comprehensive investigation into the allegations and urge firm action against those involved.
"We have submitted a memorandum to the Yang di-Pertuan Agong through His Majesty's private secretary, seeking royal intervention to urge the Madani government to establish a special tribunal," he added.
He said the proposed tribunal should investigate the matter thoroughly and identify any parties involved in activities that could threaten national security and sovereignty.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has vowed to take action against Rohingya refugees found violating the country's laws and regulations, including matters involving businesses and construction activities.
He recently said the issue of the increasing Rohingya population in Malaysia was discussed with Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail during a Cabinet meeting.
This follows the discovery of a 0.4ha Rohingya settlement hidden on private land near Jalan Sungai Tekali in Hulu Langat, which is believed to have existed for nearly 10 years.
The site is located about 1km from another viral settlement where
Rohingya
residents allegedly built four-storey structures.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Loke: Cabinet greenlights Malaysia Transport Safety Board, staffing talks underway
Article:
SEPANG, June 15 — The Cabinet has approved the establishment of the Malaysia Transport Safety Board (MTSB), with the Transport Ministry expecting to table the legislation in the next Parliament session, Anthony Loke said today.
Speaking to reporters after the 62nd Annual General Meeting of the Malaysian Road Safety Council (MKJR) at the Sama-Sama Hotel near KLIA, the transport minister said discussions are currently underway with the Public Service Department (JPA) on staffing matters for the proposed independent accident investigation body.
“The establishment of the Malaysia Transport Safety Board (MTSB) has already received Cabinet approval. We are currently discussing staffing matters with the Public Service Department.
“The Bill will be tabled in a future parliamentary sitting because the current sitting is prioritising amendments to the Road Transport Act,” he said.
Loke said the MTSB remains on track and will be responsible for investigating the causes of transport-related accidents across various sectors.
“MTSB remains on track and is progressing as planned. Its purpose is to investigate the causes of accidents,” he said.
He said accident investigations are already being conducted even before the board is formally established, with expertise from the ministry's Air Accident Investigation Bureau being deployed for major incidents involving other modes of transport.
“Several incidents have already been investigated by independent committees. Therefore, it is not accurate to say that no investigations take place in the absence of MTSB.
“For major or critical incidents, we conduct detailed investigations through specially established committees or task forces,” he said.
Loke added that the individuals who will eventually make up the board have already been identified.
“In fact, we have already identified the individuals who will form part of MTSB. The process will be formalised in the coming months,” he said.
Separately, Loke said the ministry plans to expand its Safe School Zone initiative after a pilot programme involving 14 schools nationwide.
The project, which introduces traffic-calming measures such as speed humps, road furniture and special road designs around schools, aims to improve safety for pupils travelling to and from school.
“One of the key initiatives is the Safe School Zone Pilot Project, which is currently being implemented in 14 schools nationwide. This year, we intend to expand the programme to more schools,” he said.
Loke said the initiative was particularly important as many of Malaysia's more than 10,000 schools are located along busy federal and state roads.
“As such, interventions are necessary to create safer zones. A Safe School Zone involves a specially designed environment outside the school.
“We install speed humps and traffic-calming measures to ensure vehicles slow down when passing schools. In these zones, the speed limit has been set at 30 kilometres per hour to enhance children's safety,” he said.
He said each Safe School Zone costs between RM200,000 and RM300,000 to implement, prompting the ministry to seek support from private companies and property developers to accelerate the programme's expansion.
“We also welcome support from the private sector. Establishing a Safe School Zone costs between RM200,000 and RM300,000 per school.
“As our funding is limited, we need additional support to expand the programme,” he said.
Loke said state governments, local authorities and corporate sponsors could play a larger role in identifying accident-prone areas and improving road safety infrastructure around schools and other high-risk locations.

Title: Malaysia to discuss in-car cameras for e-hailing drivers after assault cases rise
Article:
SEPANG, June 15 — The Transport Ministry is weighing new safeguards to shield e-hailing drivers from passenger violence, even as it doubles down on a strict crackdown against transport firms that flout safety standards.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke confirmed that the ministry will enter talks with e-hailing operators to bolster driver security. One proposal on the table is the installation of in-car cameras to act as a deterrent against assault.
“We will discuss the matter with e-hailing operators. Some operators have proposed installing cameras, similar to dashcams, to protect drivers,” Loke told reporters following the 62nd Annual General Meeting of the Malaysian Road Safety Council (MKJR) at the Sama-Sama Hotel today.
The proposal is not without its hurdles, however. Loke noted that any move to record the interior of a vehicle must be balanced against passenger privacy.
“We will explore what additional safety measures operators can implement to protect drivers. In terms of safety, both passengers and drivers are equally important.
“Passenger safety matters, and driver safety matters as well. We must strike the right balance,” he said.
The minister’s comments follow a string of recent reports involving e-hailing drivers being assaulted while on the job.
Shifting to broader road safety, Loke stood by the ministry’s aggressive oversight following a tragic weekend on the road.
A fatal crash on the Karak Highway yesterday claimed the life of a two-year-old girl after a trailer lorry, whose driver later tested positive for drugs, ploughed into several vehicles near Bentong.
Despite pushback from some industry players who claim the government’s approach is too heavy-handed, Loke insisted that firm action is non-negotiable.
“We have taken action against many companies — so much so that there have been complaints that we are being too strict. We have suspended the operations of some companies.
“Naturally, some businesses have complained that these actions disrupt their operations and supply chains,” he said.
Loke dismissed claims that authorities only take enforcement seriously in the wake of major tragedies, maintaining that the ministry’s stance has been consistently firm.
“Whenever accidents occur, there are allegations that enforcement is not taken seriously. However, we have been firm and uncompromising in our enforcement efforts. Many companies have had their operations suspended, not just individual drivers,” he said.
The ministry is now tightening the leash on transport operators. Loke promised a continued surge in audits and enforcement, particularly for firms with a history of accidents.
Since June last year, authorities have moved beyond reactive measures, conducting random audits on more than 500 transport companies regardless of their accident records.
“Previously, audits were generally conducted only after an accident occurred. We have now expanded the practice to include random audits,” he explained.
Loke then urged transport firms to move beyond the bare minimum and adopt internationally recognised ISO-based safety compliance standards.
“We are also encouraging more transport companies to adopt ISO-based safety compliance standards. They have a responsibility to ensure proper safety management and compliance,” he said.

Title: Anthony Loke: LRT3 launch date to be announced in next two weeks
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, June 15 — The long-awaited Light Rail Transit 3 (LRT3) line is ready to begin operations by the end of this month, with Transport Minister Anthony Loke saying the official launch date will be announced within the next two weeks.
Speaking to reporters after the 62nd Annual General Meeting of the Malaysian Road Safety Council (MKJR) today, Loke said trial operations for the line have gone smoothly and the system is prepared for passenger service.
“LRT3 is expected to open by the end of this month. The specific date will be announced within the next two weeks.
“Trial operations have gone well and the system is ready. We expect it to begin operations before the end of the month,” he said.
Dubbed the Shah Alam line, LRT3 was supposed to launch last year; however, due to outstanding system stability and software issues identified during the testing and commissioning (T&C) phase, the operations did not commence.
The LRT3 is a 37.8-kilometre light-rail transit project connecting Bandar Utama in Petaling Jaya/Damansara to Johan Setia in Klang.
The line traverses western Klang Valley, providing rail access to densely populated and rapidly developing areas, including Petaling Jaya, Shah Alam and Klang.

Title: Teacher remanded seven days from today in Kuala Terengganu over suspected sexual assault of 13-year-old boy
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — A male teacher in his 30s has been remanded for seven days to assist investigations into allegations of physical sexual assault involving a 13-year-old boy, police said.
The remand order was granted by the Kuala Terengganu Magistrates’ Court and runs from today until June 21, BuletinTV3 reported.
According to Kuala Terengganu district police chief Asst Comm Azli Mohd Noor, a report was lodged by the victim at 9.38am yesterday.
The victim alleged that the incident took place on June 4 at about 8am inside a hotel in Kuala Terengganu.
“Preliminary investigations found that the suspect is known to the victim and had previously been his teacher at primary school,” he said in a statement today.
Following the report, police arrested the suspect to assist in investigations.
The case is being investigated under Sections 14(a), 14(d) and 15B(b) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017.
Police are completing investigation papers before referring the case to the Deputy Public Prosecutor for further instructions.
In a related development, ACP Azli said police in Kuala Terengganu opened four investigation papers involving sexual offences against children between June 1 and 14.
He also urged parents to educate children early on sexual harassment awareness and to closely monitor their movements to prevent them from becoming victims.
“Students and parents must be brave to come forward to expose such despicable acts and not keep silent out of shame or fear,” he said.

Title: Bukit Kiara longhouse redevelopment to give 98 families free homes, part of land to be gazetted as public open space, says FT minister
Article:
